![Burnham](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/keeleysam/terraform-provider-burnham/main/assets/logo.svg)

# burnham Provider

> *"Make no little plans."* – Daniel Burnham

Burnham is a pure [provider-defined function](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/functions#provider-defined-functions) provider: a library that fills the gaps in Terraform's built-in expression language. Use it to assemble correct artifacts from Terraform data, including structured config formats, CEL and other policy-language expressions, IP and CIDR math, deterministic identifiers, and cryptographic material.

It defines no resources and no data sources, makes no network calls, and needs no configuration. Every function is pure and evaluates at plan time, so plans stay stable and the output always matches apply.

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    burnham = {
      source = "keeleysam/burnham"
    }
  }
}

# Burnham functions run at plan time and take no provider configuration.
# They assemble correct artifacts from Terraform data, filling gaps the
# built-in expression language cannot cover cleanly on its own.

locals {
  # Pretty, human-diffable JSON. Terraform's built-in jsonencode emits a single line.
  app_config = provider::burnham::jsonencode(
    {
      name     = "checkout"
      replicas = 3
      env      = { LOG_LEVEL = "info" }
    },
    { indent = "  " },
  )

  # Real CIDR set arithmetic instead of a templatefile()-driven preprocessor:
  # the first free /24 in 10.0.0.0/16, given what is already allocated.
  next_subnet = provider::burnham::cidr_find_free(
    ["10.0.0.0/16"],
    ["10.0.0.0/24", "10.0.1.0/24"],
    24,
  )

  # A stable, deterministic identifier that never churns the plan.
  service_id = provider::burnham::uuid_v5("dns", "checkout.example.com")
}
```

Functions are called as `provider::burnham::<name>(...)`. Each function has a runnable example under [`examples/`](https://github.com/keeleysam/terraform-provider-burnham/tree/main/examples).

## Provider Configuration

Burnham takes no configuration. There is no `provider "burnham" {}` block, no credentials, and no endpoints: declaring it in `required_providers` (as above) is all that is needed.

Provider-defined functions require Terraform 1.8 or later, or OpenTofu 1.7 or later.

## Function Families

The complete, per-function reference is in the sidebar. Burnham groups its functions into fifteen families:

| Family | What it provides |
|--------|------------------|
| Expression Languages | Assemble, validate, format, and evaluate CEL, Okta Expression Language, Cedar, and PromQL from HCL data. |
| Structured Data | Encode and decode CBOR, plist, KDL, INI, MessagePack, HuJSON, VDF, `.reg`, dotenv, and more, plus pretty-printed `jsonencode`. |
| Query and Patch | jq, JMESPath, and JSONPath queries, plus JSON Patch (RFC 6902) and JSON Merge Patch (RFC 7396). |
| Encoding | Hex, Base64, and Base32 codecs, plus the URL encode and decode that Terraform core lacks. |
| Compression | `base64zopfli` and `base64brotli`: tighter, deterministic drop-ins for the built-in `base64gzip`. |
| Networking | CIDR set arithmetic, IP arithmetic, NAT64, NPTv6, and IPAM helpers. |
| Cryptography | HMAC, HKDF, X.509 / CSR / ASN.1 inspection, and a deterministic ECDSA / Ed25519 signing pipeline. |
| Numerics | Statistics, math helpers, and RFC 3091 Pi digit generation. |
| Identifiers | Deterministic UUIDv5 and UUIDv7, Nano ID, and petname. |
| Text | Unicode normalization, slugify, Levenshtein distance, word wrap, and terminal rendering. |
| Geographic | Geohash and Open Location Code (Plus codes). |
| Color | Parse and reformat CSS colors, WCAG contrast and readable-text selection, distinct-color, ramp, and harmony-scheme generation, blending, OKLCh adjustment, and snap-to-nearest-in-palette. |
| Image | Render SVG to PNG at near-browser fidelity (gradients, filters, text, color emoji) via resvg-as-WebAssembly, deterministically and CGO-free. |
| Documents | Typeset Typst documents to PDF, PNG, SVG, and HTML (via Typst-as-WebAssembly), with structured HCL passed straight into the document as `sys.inputs`. |
| Regular Expressions | PCRE-flavored matching, capture, replace, and split with backreferences and lookaround (via fancy-regex-as-WebAssembly), the features Terraform's RE2 regex omits. |
